name: Centro De Apoyo Familiar - Center For Assistance Families
description_short: Non-profit community organization providing free housing counseling, financial literacy, and consumer education across Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, and Puerto Rico.
description_long:
Centro de Apoyo Familiar (CAF) was founded in 2006 in Lawrence, Massachusetts, initially to provide distressed homeowners with housing counseling services. The organization has since expanded significantly, establishing operations in Prince George's County, Maryland (2009), Virginia (2017), Washington DC (2020), and Puerto Rico (2024). CAF operates as a HUD-compliant, community-based non-profit focused on economic, social, and educational empowerment.

CAF offers a comprehensive suite of free services organized around three main pillars: Housing and Consumer Programs (foreclosure prevention, homebuyer education, financial literacy, rental counseling, credit counseling, and local consumer protection); Health and Family Programs (environmental justice, family strengthening, mental health support, elderly programming); and Workforce Development (digital literacy, CDL training, youth STEM programs, small business training). Their services explicitly state accessibility to all individuals regardless of race, sex, social or economic status, with Spanish-language support available.

CAF distinguishes itself through its dual focus on individual financial empowerment and community-level transformation. Rather than simply providing counseling, they operate hands-on community initiatives like the Community Garden Project in Woodbridge, Virginia, and maintain partnerships with faith-based organizations through their CAF Connectors Network. They also engage in systemic advocacy, with leadership participating in public hearings and policy discussions with government housing agencies.

As a genuine non-profit organization, CAF has no financial incentive to upsell or cross-sell. However, consumers should understand that the depth and availability of services may vary by location (Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, or Puerto Rico), and the organization's capacity is dependent on funding and volunteer resources rather than commercial operations.
pros: Completely free services with no hidden fees or upsells
HUD-compliant housing counseling and foreclosure prevention programs
Multi-state operation (Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, Puerto Rico) since 2006
Comprehensive financial literacy programs alongside credit counseling
Community-engaged approach including partnerships with faith-based organizations
Transparent mission focused on economic empowerment rather than profit
cons: Service availability limited to specific geographic areas (no nationwide coverage)
Dependent on non-profit funding model, which may limit resources and wait times
No indication of online or remote counseling options on website
Limited information about average counseling timelines or capacity constraints
Website does not specify counselor certifications (e.g., NFCC accreditation for credit counselors)
best_for: Low-to-moderate income homeowners facing foreclosure in MA, MD, VA, DC, or Puerto Rico
First-time homebuyers seeking free education and preparation programs
Spanish-speaking residents seeking bilingual financial and housing guidance
Community members seeking free credit counseling and consumer protection education
diagnosis: CAF is best for individuals in their service areas (Massachusetts, Maryland, Virginia, DC, Puerto Rico) seeking free, non-profit housing counseling, credit education, and financial literacy support. The main caveat is that service availability and depth depends on your specific location and the organization's capacity, so direct contact is necessary to determine what programs are currently available in your area.
services: Commercial Driver's License (CDL Class B) training
Environmental justice and capacity building initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
